title: Space Force adds cyber units to guard rocket launches
summary: The U.S. Space Force has established cyber defense squadrons at Cape Canaveral and Vandenberg Space Force Base to protect rocket launches from potential cyberattacks. This move highlights increasing concerns about malicious hacking threats in the space industry.

title: Amazon Leo targets faster deployment cadence as deadline pressure mounts
summary: Amazon aims to double its annual launch rate for its LEO broadband constellation to over 20 missions, relying on rockets that are still unproven at scale. The accelerated deployment is driven by increasing deadline pressure.

title: Parsons unveils new satellite antenna aimed at military market
summary: Parsons and Raven Defense have developed a hybrid satellite antenna combining a six-meter parabolic dish with an electronically steered phased-array feed, targeting the military market.

title: Sovereign demand and institutional capital reshape space economy
summary: Institutional capital and sovereign demand are reshaping the space economy, with large infrastructure funds showing interest in the sector. Seraphim Space CEO Mark Boggett noted this trend is boosting confidence in early-stage space startups.

title: Arianespace to launch Katalyst servicing spacecraft
summary: Arianespace will launch Katalyst Space Technologies' geosynchronous orbit servicing spacecraft aboard an Ariane 6 rocket in 2027. This marks a significant step in in-space servicing and assembly missions.

title: York Space rides defense demand as procurement changes cloud outlook
summary: York Space Systems is leveraging increased defense demand and commercial successes to navigate procurement changes in Space Development Agency-led programs. However, shifts in procurement policies may cloud its future outlook.

title: The software behind Artemis, Gateway and America’s defense in space deserves its own strategy
summary: The article highlights the importance of software in space infrastructure, including missions like Artemis and Gateway, and argues that it deserves its own strategic focus alongside physical assets like rockets and launch pads.

title: Lunar lander developers say they are ready to meet anticipated increased NASA demand
summary: Two lunar lander companies claim they are prepared to meet NASA's expected rise in lunar landing missions, though specifics on their approach remain undisclosed.

title: Swissto12 to build small optical relay GEO satellite for Space Compass
summary: Swissto12 will construct a small optical relay geostationary satellite for Space Compass using its HummingSat platform. This marks a significant step in optical communication satellite development.

title: SpaceX offers details on orbital data center satellites
summary: Elon Musk shared technical details about SpaceX's plans to deploy an orbital data center constellation. The announcement did not include financial specifics.

title: Scientists find 2 'failed stars' that may have a second chance to shine bright — by getting together
summary: Scientists discovered that two 'failed stars' (brown dwarfs) may merge to form a new star, potentially giving them a second chance to shine. This collision could trigger nuclear fusion and create a brighter stellar object.

title: 10 major differences between the 'Project Hail Mary' book and movie
summary: The article highlights ten major differences between Andy Weir's book 'Project Hail Mary' and its upcoming movie adaptation, focusing on changes made during the transition from page to screen.

title: 2nd-ever test flight of NASA's 'quiet' X-59 supersonic jet cut short by glitch
summary: NASA's X-59 supersonic jet completed its second test flight on March 20, but the flight was cut short due to a technical glitch. The 'quiet' supersonic aircraft aims to reduce sonic booms during flight.

title: Rare daytime fireball may have dropped meteorites on Texas: Here's where to find them
summary: A rare daytime fireball exploded over Texas with the force of 26 tons of TNT, potentially scattering meteorites across the region. Experts are advising where to search for the fragments.

title: Astrophotographer spies Thor's Helmet shining 15,000 light-years away in spectacular photo
summary: An astrophotographer captured a stunning image of Thor's Helmet Nebula, a cosmic formation shaped like the Norse god's helmet, located 15,000 light-years away. The nebula's structure is sculpted by a massive stellar wind bubble.

title: Triangulum Galaxy dazzles in psychedelic color | Space photo of the day for March 23, 2026
summary: Astronomers captured a vibrant new image of the Triangulum Galaxy, showcasing intricate gas clouds amid its 40 billion stars. The psychedelic color portrait was featured as the space photo of the day for March 23, 2026.

title: Northern lights dazzle worldwide as strong G3 geomagnetic storm sparks stunning show (photos)
summary: A powerful G3 geomagnetic storm caused the northern lights to be visible far beyond the poles, creating a stunning global spectacle captured in photos.

title: Are mysterious 'Little Red Dots' discovered by the James Webb Space Telescope actually baby galaxies under construction?
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has discovered mysterious 'Little Red Dots' that may be either baby galaxies or globular clusters in formation. Scientists are debating whether these objects are black holes or stellar nurseries.

title: Could our universe exist because black holes ate up all the antimatter?
summary: A new theory suggests that primordial black holes formed during the Big Bang may have consumed antimatter, leading to the dominance of matter in the universe. This could explain why our universe exists as we know it.

title: 10 iconic William Shatner 'Star Trek' moments to celebrate his 95th birthday
summary: A tribute article highlights ten iconic moments from William Shatner's 'Star Trek' career in celebration of his 95th birthday. The piece reflects on his enduring legacy in the sci-fi genre.

title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle Crosses Australia
summary: Tropical Cyclone Narelle impacted northern Australia with strong winds and heavy rainfall, making multiple landfalls. The storm caused significant damage along the coast.

title: NASA Data Hackathon Inspires Community Action
summary: NASA hosted an EMERGE Data Hackathon at the University of Florida to engage the community in environmental and public health science using real-world data tools. The event brought together students, researchers, and library staff to promote citizen science and education.

title: See NASA’s GUARDIAN Catch a Tsunami
summary: NASA's experimental GUARDIAN technology detects tsunamis by analyzing satellite navigation signal distortions, providing early warnings to at-risk communities. The GNSS Upper Atmospheric Real-time Disaster Information and Alert Network (GUARDIAN) offers additional lead time for disaster preparedness.

title: SWOT Mission Unlocks a New View of Our Waterways
summary: The SWOT mission has provided new insights into the movement and changes of rivers, enhancing our understanding of their role in sustaining life. This data offers a fresh perspective on Earth's waterways.

title: NASA’s Hubble, Webb Telescopes Survey Pinwheel Galaxy
summary: NASA's Hubble and James Webb Space Telescopes captured a detailed image of the Pinwheel Galaxy's core in March 2026. The galaxy, located 25 million light-years away, is one of the closest face-on spiral galaxies to Earth.

title: NASA to Provide Update on Implementation of National Space Policy
summary: NASA will host a public event to discuss its implementation of President Donald Trump's National Space Policy. The update will take place at NASA Headquarters in Washington on March 24, 2026.

title: NASA’s Hubble Revisits Crab Nebula to Track 25 Years of Expansion
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has revisited the Crab Nebula, capturing its expansion over 25 years. The new observations provide a detailed look at the supernova remnant's evolution.

title: Transformational Tools and Technologies Resources
summary: NASA has released studies and analysis documents outlining transformational tools, technologies, and resources for future space missions. These include Vision Studies, Analysis of Alternatives (AoA) Studies, and White Papers to guide strategic planning.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on the construction of launch pads at Starbase in preparation for the upcoming Block 3 Starships, highlighting their focus on increasing launch frequency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The mission follows repairs after the Soyuz MS-28 launch from the same site.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center in space.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the planned lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This brings the next launch one step closer to fruition.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is expected with nine launches scheduled, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects. The mission concept highlights Blue Origin's involvement in space-based defense technologies.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans to send humans to the Moon and Mars at its Starfactory facility, transitioning from steel production to building Starship rockets. The company's ambitious goals are highlighted in a recent update.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: The International Space Station (ISS) saw new crew arrivals and highlighted scientific activities in February after the early departure of Crew-11 in mid-January due to a medical concern.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on the latest developments in the space industry, focusing on business-to-business transactions and partnerships.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication commemorates significant milestones in space exploration and science.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft 'phoned home,' marking a significant recovery.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25). These events will highlight planned activities, thematic priorities, and industrial opportunities within ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, will launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 in March 2026, mandating its use for financial proposals in ESA tender actions. This new costing software aims to standardize financial processes for ESA suppliers.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a simpler and cheaper alternative to bulky, expensive antennas.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments. This initiative aims to advance space research and education.

title: Beyond the Rocket: The Digital Infrastructure of the Artemis II Mission
summary: The Artemis II mission's digital infrastructure is crucial but often overlooked, differing significantly from the Apollo era. NASA's digital architecture ensures the mission's success, with key contributions from Booz Allen.

title: Contrivian Launches New Product Bundling Amazon Leo & Starlink
summary: Contrivian has launched a new product that bundles Amazon's Project Kuiper (Leo) and Starlink satellite connectivity services, allowing customers to use both without choosing one over the other.

title: SpaceX v. Amazon Heats Up at the FCC
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are engaged in a regulatory dispute at the FCC regarding their satellite constellations. The public dueling highlights the competitive dynamics between the two companies in the satellite communications sector.

title: Kayhan Space Launches Satcat Terminal
summary: Kayhan Space has launched a new Satcat Terminal business, which may appeal to liberal arts majors. The initiative expands their offerings in the space industry.

title: Inside Austria’s Small But Mighty Space Ecosystem
summary: Austria's space industry is experiencing a renaissance with increased funding and startup activity. The country's small but growing ecosystem is gaining momentum.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on an operational debris removal service, emphasizing the need for active debris management to maintain critical satellite infrastructure. The initiative aims to transition debris removal from experimental to practical applications.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its SR75 suborbital rocket, with the second flight scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first satellite bus contract in Japan with NEC Corporation, which will purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission next year.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket has undergone modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster, with its launch expected this summer. These changes were planned before a recent anomaly and are now being prioritized for earlier implementation.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce reliance on satellite infrastructure. The investment highlights the growing interest in alternative navigation technologies.

title: Amazon Leo Readies 200+ Satellites for Orbit as It Ramps Up Launch Schedule
summary: Amazon's satellite division, Amazon Leo, has prepared over 200 satellites for launch at Cape Canaveral, marking a significant step in its orbital deployment plans. The company is accelerating its launch schedule to deploy these satellites in the coming months.

title: Space Force, Industry Leaders Warn of Critical Gaps in Orbital Intelligence
summary: The U.S. Space Force and industry leaders highlight significant gaps in orbital intelligence despite advanced tracking systems like ground radars and GEO spy satellites. These vulnerabilities could pose risks to space operations and national security.

title: Interview With TE Connectivity Principal Engineer Bruce Conway at SATShow Week
summary: TE Connectivity's Principal Engineer Bruce Conway discusses the company's interconnect solutions for satellites and solar panels, highlighting its deep space heritage during SATShow Week.

title: Seraphim CEO: Space Investment is at a ‘Pivotal Time’
summary: Seraphim Space CEO Mark Boggett highlighted the current space investment environment as a 'pivotal time' during a keynote at SATELLITE 2026. The industry is seen as entering a transformative phase with growing optimism.

title: Space Compass Purchases First Optical Data Relay Satellite From Swissto12
summary: Space Compass has purchased its first optical data relay satellite from Swissto12 for its planned GEO optical data relay constellation. The satellite will be part of a joint initiative with Japanese telecommunications firms.

title: Kymeta Advances Multi-Band, Multi-Orbit Antenna Toward 2027 Release
summary: Kymeta is developing the KuKa 8 Series, a multi-band, multi-orbit single aperture user terminal, with first prototypes expected in 2024. The technology aims for a 2027 release.

title: Interview With MPG Business Development Manager Dave Rawlinson at SATShow Week
summary: MPG is addressing accelerating pacing threats for government and commercial customers by scaling up delivery to meet unprecedented demand. The company's Business Development Manager, Dave Rawlinson, discussed these efforts in a video interview at SATShow Week.

title: China’s Space Development is Outpacing Expectations and Gaining Momentum, Space Force Official Says
summary: China's space development is advancing rapidly, surpassing expectations and gaining momentum as it aims to become a major space power, outpacing Russia. A U.S. Space Force official highlights the need for increased focus on this progress.

title: Spire Targets 50% Growth in 2026 After Adjustments in 2025
summary: Spire Global aims for 50% revenue growth in 2026 after divesting its maritime business and reducing debt in 2025, as announced by CEO Theresa Condor to investors.

title: Eutelsat CEO Fallacher Talks OneWeb Gen 2, Global Competition, and Sovereign Demand
summary: Eutelsat Group CEO Jean-François Fallacher discusses the company's strategic initiatives, including OneWeb Gen 2, amid global competition and sovereign demand. The interview highlights Eutelsat's transformation efforts during Fallacher's nearly one-year tenure.
